question_text: A stretched spring stores 48 J of energy in its elastic store. When released, the spring pushes a block across a rough surface. The block slides 6 metres before stopping. Explain why the spring's elastic energy does not equal the kinetic energy the block gains.
correct_answer: Some energy is dissipated (wasted) due to friction as the block slides across the rough surface, so not all the elastic energy becomes kinetic energy.
explanation: In real systems, friction and air resistance cause energy to be transferred to the thermal store (heat), which is why the block doesn't travel indefinitely. This is called energy dissipation.
